feature1.title: Live status, all your services
feature1.body: Glanceable health of every monitor your Kuma watches

feature2.title: Push notifications
feature2.body: Get pinged the second something goes down or recovers

feature3.title: Your data stays yours
feature3.body: Credentials in iOS Keychain / Android Keystore — no cloud

feature4.title: Multiple servers
feature4.body: Manage prod, staging, and client instances in one app

onboarding.hint: Self-hosted Uptime Kuma 2.0+ required. Free, MIT-licensed.

intro.title: Uptime Kuma, in your pocket
intro.body: Glanceable status, fast history, and a tap to open any monitor in its full Kuma dashboard.

privacy.title: Your data stays on your phone
privacy.body: Credentials live in the iOS Keychain or Android Keystore. We never see them — there is no cloud sync, no analytics, no account.

connect.title: Add your Kuma server
connect.body: You'll need the URL of your self-hosted Kuma instance and a bearer token (Settings → API Tokens in Kuma).

consent.title: Before you start
consent.body: Uptime Pocket talks to your own Uptime Kuma instance. We don't see that traffic, and we don't collect analytics. By continuing, you agree to our privacy policy.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.shortVersion: The short version: Uptime Pocket is a client for a server you control. We don't run a backend, we don't collect analytics, and we don't sell anything.

onDevice.title: Data stored on your device
onDevice.body: Server list, credentials (in the iOS Keychain or Android Keystore), settings, recent heartbeats, and — if you opt in — a push notification token. Nothing leaves your device unless you explicitly enable it.

offDevice.title: Data sent off your device
offDevice.body: HTTPS requests to your Kuma instance (your credentials, your choice). Push tokens to APNs / FCM (only if you enable notifications). Crash reports to our Sentry endpoint (only if you opt in to crash reports).

control.title: Your control
control.body: You can delete any server, revoke push tokens, opt out of crash reports, and remove all local data by uninstalling the app. We hold no data on our side, so there is nothing to export or delete on a server.

empty.title: No monitors yet
empty.body: Add a Kuma server to start monitoring.

notFound.title: Monitor not found
notFound.body: This monitor may have been removed or the connection was lost.

list.title: Servers
list.longPressHint: Long-press a server to make it the active one.

edit.title: Edit server
edit.secretsHint: Leave a secret blank to keep the existing one.

notFound.title: Server not found
notFound.body: This server may have been removed.

outdated.title: Outdated Kuma version
outdated.body: Detected {version}. We recommend Kuma {min} or later for the best experience.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete server?
deleteConfirm.body: Delete "{name}" and its stored credentials? This cannot be undone.

add.title: Add Kuma server
add.name: Name
add.bearerHint: Kuma 2.0+ supports API tokens. Create one in Settings → API Keys in Kuma's dashboard.

error.outdatedKuma: Kuma version {version} is below the minimum (2.0.0). Some features may not work.

mode.title: Notification mode
mode.noneHint: No notifications, app is a pure viewer
mode.directHint: App keeps a connection to Kuma, local notifications only
mode.relayHint: Full push notifications, requires the optional relay

permission.title: Stay in the loop
permission.body: Uptime Pocket can ping you the moment a monitor goes down or recovers. You'll get a system notification — no servers, no signup.
permission.laterHint: You can change this anytime in Settings.

copy.criticalBody: Multiple monitors are down at once — check your infrastructure.

theme.title: Theme
theme.descriptionSystem: Follows your iOS or Android system setting.

accentSwatch.title: Accent
accentSwatch.description: Used for buttons, links, and the up indicator on monitors.

biometric.title: Lock with biometrics
biometric.description: Require Face ID, Touch ID, or fingerprint to open the app

language.title: Language
language.description: Choose the display language. Defaults to your system language.

quietHours.title: Quiet hours
quietHours.description: Silence notifications during a set time range. Useful for overnight.

crashReports.title: Send anonymous crash reports
crashReports.description: Help improve Uptime Pocket by sending anonymous crash and error reports. No server URLs, monitor names, or account info are sent — see the privacy notice for the full list.
crashReports.privacyNoticeBody: When this is on, the app sends crash reports and error traces to our error-reporting service. We do not include server URLs, monitor names, authentication tokens, or any data that could identify your infrastructure. You can turn this off at any time.
crashReports.requiresRebuildNotice: Crash reports require a DSN to be configured at build time. If this toggle has no effect, the build you are running was not configured for crash reporting.
crashReports.dsnNotConfiguredBody: This build was not configured with a crash-reporting endpoint. The toggle is here so you can record your preference for the next time you install a configured build.

lock.title: Uptime Pocket is locked
lock.body: Unlock to view your monitors and notifications.
lock.bodyWithBiometry: Authenticate with {biometry} to view your monitors and notifications.
lock.unavailableTitle: Biometric lock is on, but unavailable
lock.unavailableBody: This device has no biometric or device-credential set up. Open Settings to disable the lock.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete monitor?
deleteConfirm.body: Delete "{name}" from {server}? This will stop all checks for this monitor and cannot be undone.

monitorDetail.noIncidents: No incidents in the last 24 hours.

validation.intervalMin: Interval must be at least 20 seconds

error.notConnected: Server is not connected. Reconnect and try again.
